steps.title: Find the plausible percent range
steps.content:
The estimate is $40\%$ with a margin of error of $4\%$, so a plausible population percentage is:
$$40\%-4\%\le p\le 40\%+4\%$$
$$36\%\le p\le 44\%$$

steps.title: Convert to people
steps.content:
Apply that range to the population of $60{,}000$:
$$0.36(60{,}000)=21{,}600$$
$$0.44(60{,}000)=26{,}400$$A plausible count must be between $21{,}600$ and $26{,}400$.

steps.title: Choose the plausible count
steps.content: The only listed value in that range is $24{,}600$. The answer is C.
